body.title: [sprint] Extend proof-on-close to the 'already built / duplicate' disposition — no grep-verified cancels
body.intention: I intend that a ticket cannot be closed or cancelled as 'already built / already done / duplicate of existing code' on the strength of a grep or a read-assertion — the close must cite a runnable proof that is RED when the claimed capability is absent or hollow, exactly as a done-close must.
body.description:
Proof-on-close (D-proof-on-close-2026-06-20) gates a close that claims DONE: emit a red->green proof or close shipped-unproven naming the missing lever. But a close/cancel whose disposition is 'this is ALREADY BUILT / duplicate of existing code / won't-do-because-it-exists' slips the gate entirely — it is an unproven ASSERTION that the code already does the thing, and that assertion can be flat wrong.

ROOT INCIDENT: T-inference-policy-router was cancelled 2026-06-23 with the reason 'rules_engine.route() already does this.' The source decision's own note admits 'grep-not-read produced a wrong premise.' The pure-policy-router / stack decomposition was NEVER implemented; the fragile hardcoded-triple rules list survived posing as the finished design (see D-inference-router-stack-decomposition-2026-07-08, filed 2026-07-08, which is now rebuilding it). A grep said the symbol existed; nobody READ that it did the work. The whole 4-stack redesign is the cost of that ungated cancel.

PROPOSED SHAPE: treat 'already-exists' as a proof-bearing disposition. To close/cancel a ticket claiming the work already exists, the close must cite a RUNNABLE existence-proof: a test that PASSES because the claimed capability is present AND is demonstrably RED when it is absent/hollow (the counterfactual — delete/stub the symbol, the test fails). Grep output and 'I read the code and it looks done' are explicitly insufficient. If such a proof cannot yet be produced, the honest path is close shipped-unproven naming the missing lever (mirroring the done-close rule) — never a bare 'already built' assertion.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (the close gate — cmd_close, and the cancel path via cmd_setstatus/block that reaches 'closed'/'cancelled'); the proof-gate logic it calls (proof_store.py / proof_emitter.py) — add or recognize an 'existence proof' mode where the counterfactual-absent run is the red form; the /sprint-ticket close-step doc + any close-disposition handling.
**Design rules:** proof-on-close (extend, don't fork it); test-plan-or-why-not; CP1 epistemic-honesty (grep-not-read is exactly the confabulation CP1 fights); no-escape-hatches (an ungated disposition IS an escape hatch).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the close/cancel gate for the 'already-exists / duplicate / already-built' disposition, plus an existence-proof mode so the counterfactual-absent run gives a real red. OUT — the done-close proof path (already gated); no new ticket statuses; not re-litigating already-closed tickets (forward-looking gate only).
**Test plan:** (1) a close with an 'already-built' reason and NO runnable proof is REFUSED by the gate (red->green on the gate logic). (2) an existence-proof where the symbol is present passes, and the same proof with the symbol stubbed/removed goes RED (proves the counterfactual actually bites). (3) shipped-unproven remains available as the honest fallback when no existence-proof is producible.
**Rollback:** the gate change is localized to the close path; revert the cc_queue close-gate commit to restore prior behavior. No data migration.
**Logging:** log a refused already-built close at WARNING (a blocked state-change is a signal); log an accepted existence-proof close at INFO with the proof id.
**Observability:** attempting `cc_queue.py close <id> --already-built` (or equivalent) without a bound existence-proof prints a refusal naming the missing proof; with one, the close records the proof id — both runtime-observable.
**Depends on:** none (independent of the router build; this is the systemic guard so it can't recur).

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
